page: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache per processor. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type includes 3780 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. In multi-core and memory benchmarks, the D64pds_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also achieves top-tier memory bandwidth for both small and large block read-only operations, alongside high-speed write capabilities.

The hardware profile delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, though single-core performance remains in the average tier. The server is highly optimized for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, as evidenced by top-tier scores in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and Clang compilation benchmarks. It is well-suited for scale-out enterprise applications, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processors running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights its strength in multi-threaded operations, software compilation, and database tasks, with top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. While single-core performance is average, its high resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for scale-out workloads. It is highly suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and web workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v6 is a 64-core ARM virtual machine with 256 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, built for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.

256 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
